item.title: Google to allow AI opt-out to ease UK competition concerns
item.description: Google said on Wednesday it вҖӢwas developing new search controls to let websites opt out of вҖҢits generative AI features, as the U.S. tech giant sought to address the British competition regulator's concerns about its dominance in search services.

item.title: X down for thousands of users in the US, Downdetector shows
item.description: Social media вҖӢplatform X was вҖҢdown for thousands of users in the U.S. вҖӢon Wednesday, according вҖӢto Downdetector.com.

item.title: Telegram still not complying with Russian law, Russian regulator says
item.description: Telegram messaging app is still вҖӢnot complying with вҖҢRussian laws, the state communications regulator Roskomnadzor said вҖӢon Wednesday, вҖӢaccording to stateвҖ‘run news agency вҒ RIA.

item.title: US factory orders barely rise in January
item.description: New orders for U.S. factory goods edged up in January as weakness in transportation equipment partially offset gains elsewhere, government data showed on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran war energy shock sparks global push to reduce fossil fuel dependence
item.description: The energy shock from the Iran war has policymakers around the globe rethinking ways to reduce long-term dependence on oil and gas imports, with proposals to expand nuclear energy and renewables, grow strategic stockpiles and domestic production, and diversify foreign sources of supply.

item.title: EU lawmakers support ban on AI apps generating explicit images
item.description: Key EU lawmakers, who are influential in shaping changes to Europe's AI Act, on вҖӢWednesday backed a ban on AI apps which вҖҢcreate unauthorised sexually explicit images and urged that the bloc's landmark rules agreed two years ago include this ban.

item.title: Streaming boosts global music revenues once again in 2025, report shows
item.description: Boosted by paid streaming services, global recorded music revenues grew 6.4% to $31.7 billion вҖҢin 2025, their 11th straight year of growth, the IFPI organisation representing the recording industry worldwide said on Wednesday, while also urging action on streaming fraud.

item.title: US waives sanctions on deals involving Venezuela's PDVSA
item.description: The вҖӢUnited States has issued a general вҖҢlicense authorizing certain deals involving Venezuela's state oil company PDVSA, the U.S. Treasury вҖӢDepartment said on Wednesday.

item.title: Researchers uncover iPhone spyware capable of penetrating millions of devices
item.description: A powerful software exploit capable of вҖӢpenetrating and stealing information from potentially hundreds of millions of Apple iPhones was planted вҖҢon dozens of websites in Ukraine in recent weeks, researchers said on Wednesday.

item.title: GM says gas price hikes not impacting sales
item.description: General Motors hasn't seen much вҖӢshift in sales following gas вҖҢprice increases as a result of the war in Iran, the вҖӢautomaker's finance chief said on вҖӢWednesday.

item.title: UniCredit CEO says pan-European ambitions take precedence over Italian consolidation
item.description: A potential combination with Commerzbank is currently the priority for UniCredit though further consolidation in Italy could still provide opportunities in вҖӢthe future, CEO Andrea Orcel said on Wednesday.

item.title: Belgium's imec secures rare ASML High NA EUV tool to drive next-generation chips
item.description: Belgian chip research lab imec said вҖҢon Wednesday it has secured an ASML $400-million High NA EUV lithography machine--one of fewer than a dozen worldwide--cementing its role in preparing next-generation chipmaking tools for industry вҖӢuse.

item.title: US producer prices surge in February on services
item.description: U.S. producer prices increased more than expected and could accelerate further as the war in the Middle East boosts oil prices вҖӢand the import pass-through persists.

item.title: Electronics firm Jabil raises annual forecasts on data center demand
item.description: Electronic component maker Jabil raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts on Wednesday, betting on вҖӢstrong demand for infrastructure services from AI data centers.

item.title: BMW eyes stabilisation in China with latest 'Neue Klasse' launch
item.description: Prices are stabilising in China after a period of heavy discounting in the electric вҖӢvehicle market, BMW sales chief Jochen Goller said вҖҢon Wednesday.

item.title: ECB sees underpriced geopolitical risks, warns against easing bank rules
item.description: Financial markets are underpricing geopolitical risks, increasing the potential for sudden sell-offs, European Central Bank supervisor вҖӢClaudia Buch said on Wednesday, as she warned вҖҢagainst easing bank regulations.

item.title: Fed's Treasury bill buying on track to moderate, amid work to rejigger bond holdings
item.description: The Federal ReserveвҖҷs work to manage challenges around its balance sheet is proving to be a rare island of calm as war and broader economic issues complicate the monetary policy outlook.

item.title: UK to examine labelling AI content among wider copyright reforms
item.description: Britain plans to consider requiring labels on AI-generated content to protect consumers from disinformation and deepfakes, the government said on вҖӢWednesday, as it outlined other areas of focus to tackle вҖҢthe evolving global challenge.

item.title: Poland plans to ban mobile phone use by under-16s in schools
item.description: Poland plans to ban the use of mobile phones by вҖӢunder-16s in schools from September 1, вҖҢthe education minister said on Wednesday, joining a growing list of countries moving to limit вҖӢchildren's screen time and social вҖӢmedia use.

item.title: EU Inc proposal seeks to rival US in innovation by easing startup creation
item.description: The European Commission proposed on вҖӢWednesday allowing firms to set up in as little as 48 hours and operate according to a вҖҢsingle set of rules across the 27-nation EU in a bid to narrow the gap with the United States in innovative startups.

item.title: General Mills reaffirms full-year sales, profit forecasts
item.description: General Mills reaffirmed its annual sales and profit forecast вҖӢon Wednesday after slashing it вҖҢlast month, as pressure on consumer spending and stiff competition muted demand for the вҖӢCheerios maker's pantry staples and вҖӢsnacks.

item.title: US to sell oil and gas leases in Alaska petroleum reserve for first time since 2019
item.description: The U.S. government on Wednesday will hold a sale of oil and gas drilling rights in Alaska's National Petroleum вҖӢReserve for the first time since 2019, the latest test вҖҢof the industry's appetite for acreage in the state.

item.title: Macy's forecasts weak annual profit; sees tariff relief
item.description: Macy's on Wednesday forecast weakerвҖ‘thanвҖ‘expected annual profit as shoppers rein in spending, though the U.S. department store chain said the вҖӢhit from import tariffs should ease in the second half вҖҢof the year.

item.title: Morning Bid: Fed plot unfolds
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: UK watchdog tightens cyber incident reporting rules as attacks surge
item.description: Britain's finance regulator confirmed вҖӢnew incident and вҖҢthird-party reporting rules on Wednesday, giving вҖӢfirms 12 вҖӢmonths to prepare for вҒ clearer requirements вҖӢaimed at strengthening вҖӢresilience against cyber attacks and third-party disruptions.

item.title: Greek firms scan computer systems as Iran war raises cyberattack risks, sources say
item.description: Greek shipowners and other companies are scanning their computer systems вҖҢfor evidence of cyberattacks after advice from the National Cybersecurity Authority, two sources said on Wednesday following incidents that have been linked to the Iran war.

item.title: Lululemon CEO void keeps investors on edge as Levi's veteran tapped for board
item.description: The appointment of Levi's veteran Chip Bergh to Lululemon's board did little to reassure Wall Street investors who are still awaiting a permanent CEO to repair the brand and вҖӢnavigate a proxy battle with founder Chip Wilson.

item.title: US hosts critical minerals event in Brazil amid diplomatic strains
item.description: The U.S. embassy will sponsor an event in Sao Paulo between U.S. investors and Brazilian firms looking to produce critical minerals on Wednesday amid diplomatic tensions that threaten to overshadow talks вҖӢto firm up Western Hemisphere supply chains.

item.title: Disney's new CEO Josh D'Amaro steps up for a wild ride
item.description: Josh D'Amaro officially assumes his new role as Disney's chief executive officer at Wednesday's annual shareholder meeting, taking the helm of the entertainment colossus at a time of profound change.

item.title: US oil loan from emergency reserve depends on stiff premiums
item.description: A U.S. plan to help control global oil prices with a swap of millions of barrels of oil from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve depends on energy companies returning oil back to the facility with вҖӢhigh premiums in the form of additional barrels, which some traders said could deter participation.
